terms.definition: Supervised binary (or multi-class) classification model: P(y=1|x) = σ(wᵀx + b), trained by minimizing binary cross-entropy. Canonical home for sigmoid + BCE in classical ML.
σ(z) = 1/(1+e^(−z)), mapping real logits to probabilities in (0, 1). Canonical definition in this topic.
The unbounded linear score z = wᵀx + b before the sigmoid; equals log(ŷ/(1−ŷ)), the log-odds of class 1.
log(p/(1−p)) for probability p; logistic regression assumes log-odds are linear in features: log(ŷ/(1−ŷ)) = wᵀx + b.

Classification loss −y log ŷ − (1−y) log(1−ŷ) for binary labels and sigmoid probability ŷ; Bernoulli form of H(P, Q). Canonical sigmoid pairing defined in this topic; general formula also in Loss Functions.
The set of x where P(y=1|x) = 0.5, equivalently wᵀx + b = 0 — a linear hyperplane separating predicted classes.
Cutoff on predicted probability ŷ to assign a class label; default 0.5 for binary logistic regression.
